title: List Page Pattern — NXGN Actions Web
purpose: AI Summary: Admin list pages use nxgn-content, nxgn-grid-page, nxgn-grid-page-title, and nxgn-server-grid bound to a server-side grid-list endpoint. Add/Edit navigation goes to routed capture pages. List pages do not open create/edit modals and do not implement client-side paging over all rows.

examples.name: Lifecycle Example 1
examples.code:
Route activates (/admin/actions)
  │
  ├─ Component initializes
  │   ├─ loading.set(true)
  │   └─ calls load()
  │
  ├─ load() → featureService.getGridList(page, pageSize)
  │   ├─ dataItems.set(result.items)
  │   ├─ totalCount.set(result.totalCount)
  │   └─ loading.set(false)
  │
  ├─ Grid renders with [dataItems] and [columnDefs]
  │
  ├─ User interacts:
  │   ├─ Selects rows → selectionChanged → selectedItems.set(...)
  │   ├─ Double-clicks row → navigate to capture page (edit)
  │   ├─ Clicks Add → navigate to capture page (new)
  │   ├─ Clicks Edit → navigate to capture page (selected item)
  │   └─ Clicks Delete → confirm → service.delete() → reload()
  │
  └─ After mutation (delete, domain command) → reload grid

examples.name: File Structure Example 1
examples.code:
modules/admin/actions/
├── action.service.ts          # HTTP calls (see Feature Service pattern)
├── action.models.ts           # DTOs and request/response interfaces
├── action-list.component.ts   # This pattern — grid + toolbar
├── action-list.component.html # Template (separate file for standalone lists)
├── action-capture.component.ts
└── ...

examples.code:
@if (errorMessage()) {
{{ errorMessage() }}
} @if (isGridDegraded()) {
{{ i18n.workflows.list.gridUnavailable | translate }}
}

rules.rule_text: Admin list pages must use nxgn-server-grid with a server-side grid-list endpoint unless the page is explicitly an embedded child picker or lookup component.
List page Add/Edit actions must navigate to routed capture pages, not open create/edit modals.
Do not implement large admin lists by loading all rows client-side and paging/filtering in the browser.

sections.title: Prerequisites
content: These packages and local helpers must be available before implementing a standalone list page. If they are missing, add them first. See [Overview - Required Packages](overview.md#required-packages) for setup instructions.

| `core/grid/server-grid` | `SERVER_GRID_DEFAULT_COL_DEF`, `SERVER_GRID_PAGE_SIZE`, `SERVER_GRID_PAGE_SIZE_SELECTOR`, `ServerGridSelection`, `getSelectedServerRows`, `tenantScopedServerGridFields` | Shared server-grid defaults and selection helpers |

sections.title: Current Server Grid-List Standard
content: New admin list pages must use nxgn-server-grid with a feature-owned grid-list endpoint constant from core/grid/grid-endpoints.ts. The page shell uses nxgn-content nxgn-grid-page and nxgn-grid-page-title. The Add action navigates to /admin/<module>/new and row double-click/Edit navigate to /admin/<module>/{id}. Large lists use server-side paging, sorting, search, and filters through the grid-list endpoint.

sections.title: Lifecycle
content: |-- Feature route array loads the list leaf component
|-- nxgn-server-grid POSTs to the configured grid-list endpoint
|     |-- request includes page size, sort/search state, and extraRequestFields
|-- Grid renders server-returned rows and total count
|     |-- Double-clicks row -> navigate to capture page (edit)
|     |-- Clicks Add -> navigate to capture page (new)
|     |-- Clicks Edit -> navigate to capture page (selected row)
|     |-- Clicks Delete -> confirm -> feature service delete command -> grid.refresh()
|-- Tenant changes or mutation completes -> clear selection and refresh server grid

sections.title: File Structure
content: Each list page lives in the feature's folder alongside its service, models, and capture page:
├── action.service.ts          # HTTP calls (see Feature Service pattern)
├── action-list.component.html # Template (separate file for standalone lists)

sections.title: Template Structure
content: | **Content shell** | Wrap standalone admin lists in `nxgn-content nxgn-grid-page` |
| **Server grid endpoint** | Bind `[endpoint]` to a `GRID_ENDPOINTS` constant |
| **Multi-select when toolbar actions need it** | `[multiSelect]="true"` enables selection for Edit/Delete |
| **Bind selectedItems to toolbar** | Controls Edit/Delete disabled state automatically |
| **Always use translate pipe** | Titles, alerts, button labels, empty states, and status text |
| **Grid degraded state** | Disable toolbar actions when `grid()?.persistentErrorMessage()` is present |
| **Embedded exception** | Use `nxgn-data-grid` only for child collections inside capture pages |

sections.title: Component Structure
content: import { ChangeDetectionStrategy, Component, computed, inject, signal, viewChild } from '@angular/core';
import { type ColDef, type RowDoubleClickedEvent } from 'ag-grid-community';
readonly selectedItems = computed(() => this.selectedRows().map(row => ({ id: row.id })));

sections.title: Grid Item DTO
content: Grid item DTOs are flat projections — counts instead of child arrays, `EnumDTO` for classification fields. See [Feature Service — Model Rules](feature-service.md) for full conventions.
evidenceCount: number;        // Flat count, not evidence[]
// ✗ WRONG — bare string has no type safety
status: string;               // ← violates ADR-0005, what values are valid?
// ✗ WRONG — bare GUID for FK references
workflowId: string;           // ← violates ADR-0005, can't display name without extra lookup
workflow: NamedDTO;           // { id: "guid", name: "Safety Inspection", description: null }

sections.title: Column Definitions
content: Because classification fields are `EnumDTO` (not bare strings), column definitions must extract `.name` for display and use `.name` for styling:
// ✓ CORRECT — extract EnumDTO.name for display and styling
// ✗ WRONG — treats status as bare string (breaks with EnumDTO)
FK reference columns use `valueGetter` to extract the display name:
// Count column (from flat grid item DTO)
Column headers should use `TranslateService` with async initialization, matching Capstone's pattern:
// Acceptable for now — hardcoded column headers (translate when i18n keys exist)

sections.title: Key Rules
content: | **Track selection as `signal<{ id: string }[]>`** | Consistent shape for toolbar binding |
| **Use `selectionChanged` event** | Emits the full selected row array |
| **Map rows to `{ id }` objects** | `rows.map(r => ({ id: r.id }))` |
| **Delete requires at least 1** | Check `selected.length === 0` and return early |
// ✓ CORRECT — map to { id } for toolbar binding
// ✗ WRONG — passing full DTOs to toolbar (leaks domain details)
| **Double-click → edit** | Navigate to capture page with entity ID |
| **Always child routes, never modals** | Consistent navigation pattern |
// ✗ WRONG — opening a modal for edit
| **Translated messages** | Confirmation title and message through `TranslateService` |
| **Reload after delete** | Call `load()` to refresh the grid from the server |
| **Aggregate roots use bulk delete** | `POST /delete` with `{ entityKeys: string[] }` |
// ✓ CORRECT — full delete flow: confirm → API → toast → reload
// ✗ WRONG — browser confirm() instead of ConfirmationDialogService
| **Returns `GridListResponse<T>`** | `{ items: T[], totalCount: number, page: number, pageSize: number }` |
| **Grid list uses POST to `/grid-list`** | POST with filter payload, not GET with query params |
| **Set loading before/after** | `loading.set(true)` in try, `loading.set(false)` in finally |
| **Reload after any mutation** | Delete, domain commands — always call `load()` |
| **`async/await` for API calls** | Components never handle Observables directly |

sections.title: Filtering
content: Some list pages support filtering via query parameters (e.g., actions filtered by workflow, evidence filtered by action ID). The filter value comes from `ActivatedRoute.queryParams`.
The template shows a filter banner when a filter is active:
<div class="alert alert-info d-flex align-items-center justify-content-between mb-3">

sections.title: Pagination
content: Grid lists support server-side pagination. The default is page 1, pageSize 25. Page state is tracked via signals and synced to query params for back-navigation preservation.
// Page change handler — update signal and reload
to: page() * pageSize() > totalCount() ? totalCount() : page() * pageSize(),
| < 100 rows typical | Load all with `page=1, pageSize=100` — no pagination UI |
| 100+ rows typical | Full pagination with page/pageSize signals |
| Audit logs, history | Always paginate — unbounded data |
Most admin entity lists (actions, workflows, compliance profiles) start with the simple `page=1, pageSize=100` approach. Add pagination when the entity count is expected to exceed 100 rows.

sections.title: Variant: Read-Only List
content: Some standalone list pages are read-only - no Add/Edit/Delete. Hide toolbar buttons, omit multi-select, and still use the server grid.
No `[multiSelect]`, no Add/Edit/Delete outputs, and no `selectionChanged` handler.

sections.title: Variant: Action-Per-Row List
content: Some list pages have per-row actions instead of (or in addition to) toolbar actions. Consultations support single-item resolution. The grid uses `(rowClicked)` to trigger inline actions.

sections.title: Notification Rules
content: | Domain command error | `ToastrService.error()` | "Failed to resolve consultation" |
| Delete confirmation | `ConfirmationDialogService.confirmDelete()` | "Delete 2 selected action(s)? This cannot be undone." |
All notification messages **MUST** go through `@ngx-translate/core` for i18n support.

sections.title: Departures from Capstone
content: | **Service location** | Centralized `GridPageService` plus feature data services in `core/services/` | Feature-colocated `{entity}.service.ts` | [ADR-0007](../../adr/0007-feature-colocated-services-on-web.md) - mirrors API vertical slices |
| **DI style** | Constructor injection | `inject()` function | Modern Angular standalone convention |

| **State management** | Plain properties (`isLoaded`, `dataItems`, `selectedItems`) | Signals (`signal()`, `computed()`) plus server-grid component state | [ADR-0006](../../adr/0006-signal-based-state-over-behaviorsubject.md) - reactive state |
| **Change detection** | Default zone-based | `OnPush` with signal-driven state | Signal-based state enables predictable rendering |

| **Data loading** | Component/service manually calls `GetGridList` and binds local arrays | `nxgn-server-grid` POSTs to a `/grid-list` endpoint from `GRID_ENDPOINTS` | Server-side paging/search/sort remains inside the grid contract |
| **Delete confirmation** | `window.confirm()` with translated string | `ConfirmationDialogService.confirm()` or `confirmDelete()` | Styled modal consistent with app design |
| **Delete error handling** | Partial success via response arrays | Full success/failure; refresh grid after success or error | Actions API commands are transactional |

| **Selection tracking** | `@ViewChild` plus imperative `getSelectedNodes()` | `(selectionChanged)` event -> `getSelectedServerRows()` -> `signal<DTO[]>()` | Declarative signal flow avoids leaking grid internals |
| **Standalone grid component** | `NxgnGridComponent` or local client-side wrappers | `ServerGridComponent` / `nxgn-server-grid` | Shared server-side grid contract |
| **Embedded child grid component** | Same grid primitive as list pages | `DataGridComponent` / `nxgn-data-grid` only inside capture pages | Child rows are local aggregate state until parent Save |
| **Toolbar component** | `EntityGridPageTitleComponent` with legacy projection | `GridPageTitleComponent` with named projection | Shared UI library plus modern control flow |
| **Grid resize** | Manual window resize handling | Handled internally by the grid component | Shared grid component owns layout behavior |

| **Column translation** | `await lastValueFrom(translate.get('key'))` in `configureColumns()` | Translation keys come from the shell contract and refresh through signals where needed | Keeps Actions translation ownership centralized |
| **Observable handling** | `lastValueFrom()` | `firstValueFrom()` or signal interop | HTTP emits once; signal interop handles reactive shell state |

| **Add/Edit navigation** | `Router.navigate([captureRoute], { state: captureCriteria })` with `IEntityCaptureCriteria` | `Router.navigate(['/admin/feature', id])`; route param determines mode | Deep-linkable routes and simpler state |

sections.title: See Also
content: - [Feature Service](feature-service.md) - Service the list page injects for commands and feature-owned HTTP calls
- [Capture Page](capture-page.md) - Form page navigated to from the list
- [Embedded List](embedded-list.md) - Child grid within a capture page using `nxgn-data-grid`
- [Capture State](capture-state.md) - State service for aggregate child routes
- [Validation](validation.md) - Confirmation dialog patterns used by delete
- [Overview](web-overview.md) - Project structure, required packages, naming conventions
